segments.text: Plead my cause, O Lord, with those who strive with me; Fight against those who fight against me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Take hold of shield and buckler, And stand up for my help.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Also draw out the spear, And stop those who pursue me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I am your salvation.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Let those be put to shame and brought to dishonor Who seek after my life; Let those be turned back and brought to confusion Who plot my hurt.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them be like chaff before the wind, And let the angel of the Lord chase them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let their way be dark and slippery, And let the angel of the Lord pursue them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For without cause they have hidden their net for me in a pit, Which they have dug without cause for my life.
Let destruction come upon him unexpectedly, And let his net that he has hidden catch himself; Into that very destruction let him fall.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And my soul shall be joyful in the Lord; It shall rejoice in His salvation.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Lord, who is like You, Delivering the poor from him who is too strong for him, Yes, the poor and the needy from him who plunders him?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Fierce witnesses rise up; They ask me things that I do not know.
They reward me evil for good, To the sorrow of my soul.
But as for me, when they were sick, My clothing was sackcloth; I humbled myself with fasting; And my prayer would return to my own heart.
I paced about as though he were my friend or brother; I bowed down heavily, as one who mourns for his mother.
But in my adversity they rejoiced And gathered together; Attackers gathered against me, And I did not know it; They tore at me and did not cease;
With ungodly mockers at feasts They gnashed at me with their teeth.
Lord, how long will You look on? Rescue me from their destructions, My precious life from the lions.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will give You thanks in the great assembly; I will praise You among many people.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them not rejoice over me who are wrongfully my enemies; Nor let them wink with the eye who hate me without a cause.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For they do not speak peace, But they devise deceitful matters Against the quiet ones in the land.
Aha, aha! Our eyes have seen it.
segments.speaker: David's Enemies

segments.text: This You have seen, O Lord; Do not keep silence. O Lord, do not be far from me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Stir up Yourself, and awake to my vindication, To my cause, my God and my Lord.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Vindicate me, O Lord my God, according to Your righteousness; And let them not rejoice over me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them not say in their hearts, 'Ah, so we would have it!' Let them not say, 'We have swallowed him up.'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them be ashamed and brought to mutual confusion Who rejoice at my hurt; Let them be clothed with shame and dishonor Who exalt themselves against me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them shout for joy and be glad, Who favor my righteous cause; And let them say continually, 'Let the Lord be magnified, Who has pleasure in the prosperity of His servant.'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And my tongue shall speak of Your righteousness And of Your praise all the day long.
segments.speaker: David
